title: Signatures
description: Sync Microsoft 365 staff, manage templates, and push Outlook signatures.

disabled.title: Signatures isn’t enabled
disabled.description: Turn on the Signatures module for this workspace in account settings (or upgrade billing if your plan includes add-on modules).

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Monitor staff signature status and push current templates to Outlook.
dashboard.sectionStaffDescription: A quick view of synced staff, assigned templates, and the last push state.

staff.title: Staff
staff.subtitle: Review synced Microsoft 365 staff and filter by branch, department, or push status.

staffDetail.subtitle: Edit staff profile details, assign a template, and preview the rendered Outlook signature.

templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: Create and edit reusable HTML email signature templates.
templates.noTemplates: No templates yet. Create a default template to get started.

templateEditor.title: HTML template
templateEditor.tokenPanelHint: Click a token to insert it at the cursor.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage the Microsoft 365 connection used by the signatures module.
settings.notConnectedDescription: Connect Microsoft 365 to sync staff and push Outlook signatures.

connectionGate.title: Connect Microsoft 365
connectionGate.description: Signatures needs a Microsoft 365 connection before staff can be synced and email signatures can be pushed to Outlook mailboxes.

staffEditor.photoHint: Uploading replaces the synced photo for this staff profile.
staffEditor.previewAssignTemplate: Assign a template to preview this signature.
staffEditor.pushDialogDescription: Ozer will send the currently assigned template to this staff member’s Microsoft mailbox.

emptyStates.noStaff: No staff synced yet. Connect Microsoft 365, then sync staff from M365.
emptyStates.staffTable: No staff synced yet. Connect Microsoft 365, then sync staff from M365.

errors.postgrestSchemaDescription: Your Supabase project must expose the database schema named signatures to PostgREST. Local dev uses supabase/config.toml; hosted projects need a Dashboard setting.
errors.postgrestSchemaStep1: Open Supabase Dashboard → your project → Settings → API.
errors.postgrestSchemaStep2: Under Exposed schemas, add signatures (keep public and any others you already use). Save.
errors.postgrestSchemaStep3: Apply migrations that create the signatures schema and tables (for example supabase db push), then reload this page.
errors.moduleDisabled: Signatures is disabled for this account.
